theme_notes: Lines drawn from the US-Iran war on day 61 (gas above $4/gallon, Strait of Hormuz blockade, Pentagon's $25bn tab vs. economists' $1 trillion) and the Supreme Court effectively gutting the Voting Rights Act Section 2; seasoned with FIFA's new red-card rule ahead of World Cup 2026.
settled: All even. Which is more than anyone can say about Iran right now.
Zero. The Supreme Court strikes down a lot of things. Not this.
Balanced. FIFA would give you a green card if that were a thing.
youOwe: Less than a gallon of premium in the war economy. Somehow still shameful.
The Strait of Hormuz is less of a bottleneck than your shame.
FIFA made the new red card rule specifically for this.
One fill-up at war prices. Congratulations on your worst financial milestone.
The World Cup starts this summer. Your debt will outlast it.
The Voting Rights Act lasted 61 years. Pay before your excuses don't.
The Iran war spends this every four seconds. You two are kindred.
Not enough for World Cup finals tickets, but enough for permanent guilt.
Gas is $4 a gallon. This is a month of commuting in shame.
Trump told Iran to 'just give up.' Some of us are still waiting.
The Supreme Court gutted the VRA. Your debt, unfortunately, remains.
World Cup brackets are cleaner than your payment plan.
Hegseth couldn't explain the Iran war budget to Congress. Relate.
You are now a sanctioned economic actor with relationship privileges.
The World Cup has knockout rounds. You've been in one for months.
The Pentagon says the Iran war cost $25bn. You have the same commitment to escalation.
You are the Strait of Hormuz. Nothing flows in or out.
The economists say the real cost is closer to $1 trillion. So do you.
theyOwe: They owe you pocket change. In a $4/gallon world, that's still principle.
Smaller than a FIFA red card fine. Larger than acceptable.
The Iran war burns through this in a blink. Still collect it.
One tank of gas. They filled up on your credit.
The VRA is gone but receipts remain. Use them.
Enough for World Cup merch they already bought without consulting you.
The Strait of Hormuz is more accessible than their Venmo.
In the war economy, this is groceries for a month. Collect it.
Oil is at $120 a barrel. Your patience has exceeded its value.
Iran and the U.S. have more productive negotiations than you two do.
Enough to cover gas for months. They drove on your principles.
Iran is not giving up. Take notes.
The Pentagon undercounts Iran war costs. Your partner does the same.
You've been more patient than Congress at the Hegseth hearings.
At $4/gallon, this is your freedom fund. Start the engine.
The U.S. and Iran both claim $1 trillion in damages. You're in good company.
This is no longer a debt. It's an international incident.
The blockade has been in place too long. Escalate accordingly.
